Greek-Entry.English-Entry: πότνια γῆ, Ζαγρεῦ τε θεῶν πανυπέρτατε πάντων
to put forth all one's fury
the roof of a horse's mouth,
ζέλκεια: λάχανα παρὰ Φρυξὶ τὰ παρ' ἡμῖν λεγόμενα Φρύγια
διάβροχα ξύλα: καὶ τῶν βοῶν ἢ ἡμιόνων ζευκτά: καὶ τέμαχος ἐκ πλευρᾶς ἡλισμένης
the driving a yoke of oxen
the driver of a yoke of oxen, teamster
of which were made the mouthpieces of the double flutes
their being able to keep a team
that which is used for joining, band, bond
platform formed by lashing several vessels together
the making of mouthpieces for double flutes
carriage drawn by a yoke of beasts, a chariot
to be drawn by a yoke of oxen
that which is emulated: object of envy
to be deemed happy, to beenvied
have to seek, feel the want of
commissioners to inquire into extraordinary offences
to recover moneys owing to the State
a place of punishment for slaves
leader of a line of horsemen
the rower who sat on the mid-most of the three banks
tax on land at so much a jugum
one who uses a false balance
ransom paid for a prisoner taken alive) reward for life saved
connected with painting, used by painters
place for keeping animals, a menagerie
restore to life and strength, revive
giving the bloom and freshness of life
small room wherein to rest by day
the lower girdle worn by women
part round which the girdle passed, waist
anything that goes round like a belt
one of the zones of the terrestrial sphere,
to be fixed by means of girths
in the shape of an animal
mould to the life, make into statues
connected with the feeding of animals
bearing the figures of animals, sculptured
pitch and wax from old ships
kindle into flame, cause to blaze up
anything that goes round like a girdle
stripe marking certain height in the ship
the sun's progress through the zodiac
